stem:
f(x) = 9000(0.49)x/12

The function f gives the value, in dollars, of a certain piece of equipment after x months of use. If the value of the equipment decreases each year by p% of its value the preceding year, what is the value of p?
rationale:
Choice B is correct. After 12 months (one year), the multiplier is (0.49)12/12 = 0.49. So the value at the end of each year is 49% of the previous year's value, meaning it has decreased by 100% − 49% = 51%.

Choice A is the multiplier itself, not the percent decrease. Choice C is the retention rate as a percent. Choice D arises from a decimal-point error.
